elements.sent-2411-004: Integrability preserving deformations of two-dimensional $\s$-models garnered considerable attention in the literature.
elements.sent-2411-005: A notable example is the so-called $\l$-model \cite{Sfetsos:2013wia , originally formulated by gauging the combined action of a Wess-Zumino-Witten (WZW) model and a principal chiral model (PCM) for a semi-simple group $G$.}
elements.sent-2411-006: This model interpolates between an exact conformal field theory (CFT), represented by a WZW model with a current algebra symmetry at level $k$, and the non-Abelian T-dual (NATD) of the PCM for $G$.
elements.sent-2411-007: This construction can be generalised to deformations based on cosets with Lagrangian description through gauged WZW models, as well as symmetric or semi-symmetric spaces \cite{Sfetsos:2013wia,Hollowood:2014rla,Hollowood:2014qma .}
elements.sent-2411-008: A variation of the $\l$-model with spectators was derived in \cite{Borsato:2024alk by modifying the gauging procedure.}
elements.sent-2411-010: Another class of integrable $\s$-models connected to the PCM on groups, known as $\eta$-deformations, was introduced in \cite{Klimcik:2002zj,Klimcik:2008eq .}
elements.sent-2411-011: This was generalised to the context of coset spaces in \cite{Delduc:2013fga,Delduc:2013qra , inspiring a lot of activity in the framework of the AdS/CFT correspondence.}
elements.sent-2411-012: A relation between the isotropic $\l$-model and the $\eta$-model, by means of Poisson-Lie T-duality \cite{Klimcik:1995ux and appropriate analytic continuations, has been established in \cite{Vicedo:2015pna,Hoare:2015gda,Sfetsos:2015nya .}}
elements.sent-2411-013: The main objective of the present work is to build solutions of type-II supergravity out of the background fields of $\l$-deformed cosets.
elements.sent-2411-014: This is in general a non-trivial task, which requires the completion of the $\s$-model field content with appropriate Neveu-Schwarz (NS) and Ramond-Ramond (RR) fields.
elements.sent-2411-015: Nevertheless, type-II supergravity backgrounds based on $\l$- and $\eta$-deformed (super) cosets have been constructed in various works \cite{Hoare:2015gda,Demulder:2015lva,Borsato:2016zcf,Itsios:2019izt,Chervonyi:2016ajp,Borsato:2016ose,Hoare:2015wia,Hoare:2018ngg,Lunin:2014tsa,Hoare:2022asa .}

elements.sent-2411-309: Our approach relies on introducing well-motivated ansatze for the RR fields, which define the structure of the corresponding geometries.

elements.sent-2411-326: Here, we present a concise overview of the equations of motion for the type-IIA and type-IIB supergravity theories.
elements.sent-2411-327: The field content of the NS sector in type-IIA supergravity consists of the ten-dimensional metric $g_{\m\n}$, the dilaton $\Phi$ and a three-form $H$.
elements.sent-2411-328: The RR sector contains the forms $F_2$ and $F_4$, where the index indicates their rank.
elements.sent-2411-329: The form fields can be expressed in terms of the potentials $(B , C_1 , C_3)$ as \begin{equation}H = dB \, , \qquad F_2 = dC_1 \, , \qquad F_4 = dC_3 - H \wedge C_1 \, .\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-100: \begin{equation}H = dB \, , \qquad F_2 = dC_1 \, , \qquad F_4 = dC_3 - H \wedge C_1 \, .\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-330: The above imply the Bianchi identities \begin{equation}\label{BianchisIIA} dH = 0 \, , \qquad dF_2 = 0 \, , \qquad dF_4 = H \wedge F_2 \, .\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-331: The dynamics of the various fields is described by the following ten-dimensional action in the string frame \begin{equation}\label{ActionIIA} \begin{aligned} S_{IIA} = \frac{1}{2 \kappa^2_{10}} \int\limits_{M_{10}} \sqrt{- g} \Bigg[ e^{-2 \Phi} \left( R + 4 \big( \partial \Phi \big)^2 - \frac{H^2}{12} \right) - \frac{1}{2} \left( \frac{F^2_2}{2} + \frac{F^2_4}{4!} \right) \Bigg] [5pt] - \frac{1}{4 \kappa^2_{10}} \int\limits_{M_{10}} B \wedge dC_3 \wedge dC_3 \, . \end{aligned}\end{equation} Varying the action with respect to $\Phi$, we derive the equation of motion for the dilaton \begin{equation}\label{DilatonEOM} R + 4 \nabla^2 \Phi - 4 \big( \partial \Phi \big)^2 - \frac{H^2}{12} = 0 \, .\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-102: \begin{equation}\label{ActionIIA}  S_{IIA} = \frac{1}{2 \kappa^2_{10}} \int\limits_{M_{10}} \sqrt{- g} \Bigg[ e^{-2 \Phi} \left( R + 4 \big( \partial \Phi \big)^2 - \frac{H^2}{12} \right) - \frac{1}{2} \left( \frac{F^2_2}{2} + \frac{F^2_4}{4!} \right) \Bigg] [5pt] - \frac{1}{4 \kappa^2_{10}} \int\limits_{M_{10}} B \wedge dC_3 \wedge dC_3 \, . \end{equation}
elements.formula-2411-105: \begin{equation}\label{FluxesIIA}  d \big( e^{- 2 \Phi} \star H \big) - F_2 \wedge \star F_4 - \frac{1}{2} F_4 \wedge F_4 = 0 \, , [5pt] d \star F_2 + H \wedge \star F_4 = 0 \, , [5pt] d \star F_4 + H \wedge F_4= 0 \, . \end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-333: Like in type-IIA supergravity, the NS sector of the type-IIB theory contains the ten-dimensional metric $g_{\m\n}$, the dilaton $\Phi$ and a three-form $H$.
elements.sent-2411-334: However, the RR sector now contains forms of odd rank, namely the $F_1$, $F_3$ and the self-dual five-form $F_5$.
elements.sent-2411-335: Again, the index stands for the rank of the forms.
elements.sent-2411-336: The form fields can be written in terms of the potentials $(B , C_0 , C_2 , C_4)$ as \begin{equation}H = dB \, , \qquad F_1 = dC_0 \, , \qquad F_3 = dC_2 - C_0 H \, , \qquad F_5 = dC_4 - H \wedge C_2 \, .\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-106: \begin{equation}H = dB \, , \qquad F_1 = dC_0 \, , \qquad F_3 = dC_2 - C_0 H \, , \qquad F_5 = dC_4 - H \wedge C_2 \, .\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-337: The latter imply the Bianchi identities \begin{equation}\label{BianchisIIB} dH = 0 \, , \qquad dF_1 = 0 \, , \qquad dF_3 = H \wedge F_1 \, , \qquad dF_5 = H \wedge F_3 \, .\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-107: \begin{equation}\label{BianchisIIB} dH = 0 \, , \qquad dF_1 = 0 \, , \qquad dF_3 = H \wedge F_1 \, , \qquad dF_5 = H \wedge F_3 \, .\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-338: The dynamics of this field content is now described by the following ten-dimensional action in the string frame \begin{equation}\label{ActionIIB} \begin{aligned} S_{IIB} = \frac{1}{2 \kappa^2_{10}} \int\limits_{M_{10}} \sqrt{- g} \Bigg[ e^{-2 \Phi} \left( R + 4 \big( \partial \Phi \big)^2 - \frac{H^2}{12} \right) - \frac{1}{2} \left( F^2_1 + \frac{F^2_3}{3!} + \frac{F^2_5}{2 \cdot 5!} \right) \Bigg] [5pt] - \frac{1}{4 \kappa^2_{10}} \int\limits_{M_{10}} C_4 \wedge H \wedge dC_2 \, . \end{aligned}\end{equation} Since the NS sector in the type-IIB action coincides with that of the type-IIA, the equation of motion for the dilaton is still given by \eqref{DilatonEOM .}
elements.formula-2411-108: \begin{equation}\label{ActionIIB}  S_{IIB} = \frac{1}{2 \kappa^2_{10}} \int\limits_{M_{10}} \sqrt{- g} \Bigg[ e^{-2 \Phi} \left( R + 4 \big( \partial \Phi \big)^2 - \frac{H^2}{12} \right) - \frac{1}{2} \left( F^2_1 + \frac{F^2_3}{3!} + \frac{F^2_5}{2 \cdot 5!} \right) \Bigg] [5pt] - \frac{1}{4 \kappa^2_{10}} \int\limits_{M_{10}} C_4 \wedge H \wedge dC_2 \, . \end{equation}
elements.formula-2411-110: \begin{equation}\label{FluxesIIB}  d \big( e^{- 2 \Phi} \star H \big) - F_1 \wedge \star F_3 - F_3 \wedge F_5 =0\, , [5pt] d \star F_1 + H \wedge \star F_3 = 0 \, , [5pt] d \star F_3 + H \wedge \star F_5 = 0 [5pt] d \star F_5 - H \wedge F_3 = 0 \, . \end{equation}
